readings.title: An Efficient Algorithm for Exploiting Multiple Arithmetic Units
readings.note: The 1967 paper that introduced reservation stations, register renaming via tags, and the Common Data Bus. Read the original — it is only 9 pages and remarkably clear for a 60-year-old paper. Published in the IBM Journal of Research and Development.

readings.title: Spectre Attacks: Exploiting Speculative Execution
readings.authors: Paul Kocher, Jann Horn, Anders Fogh, Daniel Genkin, Daniel Gruss, Werner Haas, Mike Hamburg, Moritz Lipp, Stefan Mangard, Thomas Prescher, Michael Schwarz, Yuval Yarom
readings.note: The 2018 paper that revealed how speculative execution creates exploitable side channels. Sections 1-4 are essential reading for understanding how branch predictor mistraining leads to data exfiltration through cache timing.

readings.title: A Case for (Partially) TAgged GEometric History Length Branch Prediction
readings.note: The seminal TAGE predictor paper. Seznec's geometric history length insight — indexing predictor tables with histories that form a geometric series — became the basis for all modern high-performance branch predictors. Won CBP-2 and descendants dominated CBP-3 through CBP-5.

readings.title: Dynamic Branch Prediction with Perceptrons
readings.authors: Daniel A. Jimenez and Calvin Lin
readings.note: Introduced the perceptron branch predictor at HPCA 2001. The key insight is that a single-layer neural network (perceptron) scales linearly with history length — $n+1$ weights vs. $2^n$ table entries for traditional predictors. AMD's Zen processors use perceptron-based predictors.

readings.title: Intel Golden Cove Deep Dive — AnandTech
readings.note: Detailed analysis of Intel's Golden Cove microarchitecture used in 12th Gen Alder Lake. Covers the 6-wide decode, 512-entry ROB, 12 execution ports, and the micro-op cache. Essential for understanding modern x86 out-of-order design.

readings.title: AMD's Zen 4 Part 1: Frontend and Execution Engine — Chips and Cheese
readings.note: Microarchitectural analysis of AMD Zen 4. Covers the 4-wide decode, 6.75K op cache, 320-entry ROB, and Zen 4's integer and floating-point execution engine. Good companion to the Intel Golden Cove deep dive for cross-vendor comparison.

readings.title: Zen 5's 2-Ahead Branch Predictor Unit — Chips and Cheese
readings.note: Analysis of AMD Zen 5's innovative 2-ahead branch predictor, which predicts two taken branches per cycle by feeding one prediction into the next within the same cycle. A 30-year-old idea finally realized in silicon.

readings.title: Branch Prediction Is Not A Solved Problem: Measurements from Industrial Applications
readings.note: Measures branch prediction behavior in real server workloads. Key finding: modern server applications waste an average of 9.2% of cycles on branch mispredictions, with some workloads losing over 20% of cycles. Demonstrates that branch prediction remains a significant bottleneck.
